name: Mars Pyramids
description: The 'Mars Pyramids' theory, primarily circulating within American alternative history and conspiracy theory circles, posits that structures resembling pyramids, as well as other artificial formations, exist on the surface of Mars. Proponents, often relying on interpretations of NASA images, suggest these structures are evidence of an ancient, intelligent Martian civilization, possibly predating or even influencing human civilization on Earth. The theory gained traction with the Viking 1 orbiter images in the 1970s, particularly the 'Face on Mars' in the Cydonia region. Further images from later missions, such as Mars Global Surveyor and Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter, have fueled ongoing debate. Proponents often suggest that NASA and other space agencies are deliberately concealing evidence of these Martian structures. Mainstream science dismisses the 'Mars Pyramids' as pareidolia, a psychological phenomenon where the human brain perceives familiar patterns in random stimuli. High-resolution images and detailed topographic data from modern Mars missions have revealed that the purported pyramids are natural geological formations, such as mesas and buttes, shaped by wind and erosion over millions of years. The perceived geometric shapes are considered optical illusions caused by low resolution and lighting conditions in the original images. The theory is linked to broader themes of ancient astronauts, lost civilizations, and government cover-ups.
subtitle: Artificial structures on the Red Planet?

claims.evidence: Images from NASA missions, particularly the 'Face on Mars' and surrounding formations in Cydonia.
claims.criticism: Mainstream science attributes these formations to natural geological processes (erosion, wind) and pareidolia. High-resolution images reveal them to be mesas and buttes.

claims.claim: These structures are artificial and built by an intelligent civilization.
claims.evidence: The perceived geometric shapes and arrangement of the formations suggest intentional design.
claims.criticism: No definitive evidence of artificiality has been found. The 'geometric shapes' are subjective interpretations and can be explained by natural processes. Occam's Razor favors natural explanations.

claims.claim: NASA and other space agencies are covering up the existence of Martian civilizations.
claims.evidence: Allegations of image manipulation, suppression of research, and discrediting of proponents.
claims.criticism: This is a common conspiracy theory trope. There is no credible evidence to support a cover-up. Scientists routinely publish data and interpretations that contradict the 'Mars Pyramids' theory.

keyFigures.name: Richard Hoagland
keyFigures.role: Prominent proponent of the 'Mars Pyramids' and 'Face on Mars' theories.

keyFigures.name: Carl Sagan
keyFigures.role: Vocal skeptic and advocate for scientific reasoning regarding the 'Face on Mars' and similar claims.

keyFigures.name: Michael Shermer
keyFigures.role: Science writer and publisher of Skeptic magazine, frequently debunking pseudoscientific claims, including those related to Martian structures.

mythologyConnections: Connections to ancient pyramid building cultures (Egypt, Mesoamerica)
Martian gods in science fiction (e.g., H.G. Wells' War of the Worlds)

timeline.event: Viking 1 orbiter captures the 'Face on Mars' image.
Richard Hoagland and others popularize the 'Mars Pyramids' theory.
Higher resolution images from Mars Global Surveyor and Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter provide more detailed views of Cydonia, largely discrediting the artificiality claims.

sources.author: Hoagland, Richard
